function.description: Activates a specialized agent skill by name. Returns the skill's instructions wrapped in <skill_content> tags. These provide specialized guidance for the current task. Use this when you identify a task that matches a skill's description. ONLY use names exactly as they appear in the <available_skills> section. [Read-Only: Safe for Plan Mode]
function.name: activate_skill

name.description: The name of the skill to activate.

function.description:
Activates a specialized tool family by name. This makes all tools belonging to that family available in the agent's context. By default, only the 'base' family is active.

<registered_tool_families>
["base","x86"]
</registered_tool_families> [Read-Only: Safe for Plan Mode]
function.name: activate_tool_family

name.description: The name of the tool family to activate.

function.description: Adds a new task to the AI agent's internal todo (task) list. Use this to track steps during complex multi-part requests. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_add_todo

text.description: The description of the task to add.

function.description: Marks a task as complete in the AI agent's internal todo list. Provide an exact string match or a unique substring. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_complete_todo

text.description: The exact task text or a unique substring to match.

function.description: Proactively pages out all conversational history prior to this tool call into a saved milestone archive. This frees up your context window. A highly dense pointer message will replace the old history, allowing you to restore it later if needed. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_compress_history

include_all_prior.description: Optional. If true, ignores the lower boundary and compresses everything from the target back to the system prompt.

summary.description: A concise summary of the history being paged out. This summary will be injected into your context as the pointer.

tags.description: An optional list of semantic tags to label this archive.

target_episode_id.description: Optional. The exact ID of the milestone or system message (e.g., 'milestone_123') that acts as the UPPER boundary. If omitted, pages out the active/current block.

title.description: A short title for the milestone you are archiving.

function.description: Deletes a task from the AI agent's internal todo list. Provide an exact string match or a unique substring. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_delete_todo

text.description: The exact task text or a unique substring to match.

function.description: Retrieves the interaction history of a subagent. By default, it also terminates the agent unless 'keep' is set to true. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_get_output

id.description: The ID of the subagent to query.

keep.description: If true, the subagent is kept alive after its output is retrieved. Defaults to false (auto-terminate).

function.description: Returns a snapshot/screenshot of the terminal buffer grid, cursor coordinates, and visibility status for a given run ID. [Read-Only: Safe for Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_get_run_screenshot

run_id.description: The unique execution ID returned by agent_start_app.

function.description: Lists all archived/paged-out episodes, returning a markdown table showing the episode ID and their 'when to resume' reactivation hint. [Read-Only: Safe for Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_list_episodes

function.description: Lists all tasks currently in the AI agent's internal todo list, formatted as markdown checkboxes. [Read-Only: Safe for Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_list_todos

function.description: Used to signal that a major task is complete or that you are pivoting to a completely new area. This helps the system manage long-term memory and context windows efficiently by compressing old history. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_mark_episode

summary.description: A concise summary of the work that was just completed and the goal of the new phase.

tags.description: An optional list of semantic tags (e.g., ['ui-refactor', 'memory-leak']) to label this episode. You can assign the same tags to multiple episodes to group them together for easy bulk-restoration later.

title.description: A short title for the completed task or the new episode.

function.description: Pages in a previously saved context archive (episode). Use this if you need to resume work on an old task or look up historical context. Find the episode_id by using the '/memory' command or reading the SYSTEM MEMORY pointers in your history. [State-Modifying: Blocked in Plan Mode]
function.name: agent_restore_context

compression_level.description: Optional. 0=Raw, 1=Think-Free (default), 2=Terminal Truncated, 3=Highly Aggressive. Adjusts how much bloat is stripped from the archive before loading it into your context.

episode_id.description: The ID of the episode archive to page in (e.g., 'episode_5').

message.content:
I've successfully activated the x86 tools and inspected the ELF file at `build/turbostar`. Here's what I found:

### ELF Section Headers
The ELF file contains 40 sections, including:
- **Program sections**: `.text` (code), `.rodata` (read-only data), `.data` (data), `.bss` (uninitialized data)
- **Dynamic sections**: `.dynsym`, `.dynstr`, `.rela.dyn`, `.rela.plt`, `.plt` (procedure linkage table)
- **Debug sections**: `.debug_info`, `.debug_abbrev`, `.debug_line`, `.debug_str`, `.debug_line_str`, `.debug_rnglists`
- **Metadata sections**: `.note.gnu.build-id`, `.note.gnu.property`, `.note.ABI-tag`, `.comment`

### Symbols Containing 'dump'
I found numerous symbols containing 'dump', primarily related to:
- `crashdump_window` class (UI component for displaying crash dumps)
- `crashdump_info` structure (contains crash dump information)
- `tools::crashdump_list_tool` (tool for listing crash dumps)
- `editor::new_crashdump_window()` (function to create crash dump window)
- Various C++ standard library components for managing crashdump objects

The most significant symbols include:
- `editor::new_crashdump_window()` - Function to create a new crash dump window
- `crashdump_window` class methods (constructor, destructor, UI methods like `populate_listbox()`, `draw_content()`, `process_events()`)
- `crashdump_info` struct methods (constructor, destructor, assignment)
- `tools::crashdump_list_tool` - Implementation of the crash dump listing tool

Would you like me to investigate any specific aspect of these dump-related components?
